preamble: The shift from the voiced velar stop [ɡ] to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] (zh) is a form of palatalization followed by deaffrication. This transformation is a hallmark of historical sound changes in Slavic and certain Romance languages (notably French), where the stop fronted before high vowels or glides.

examples.note: Historical palatalization of Latin /ɡ/ before /a/ (and front vowels) in the development of French.
Reflects the Slavic First Palatalization where /ɡ/ shifted to [ʒ] before front vowels.

sources: Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic: An Introduction to Slavic Philology.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
